prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='DWT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.23) and high seasonality variance (0.62), with early horizon specialists (ONLY_DWT_svr, DWT_catboost) differing from late horizon models (FT_svr, ONLY_FT_catboost). The trend champion ONLY_FT_catboost captures downward trends best, while DWT_svr leads in seasonal correlation. Given the high disagreement and variance across models, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors without over-weighting any single approach.

You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "DWT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "FT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_svr", "late": "FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_svr", "THETA", "ETS", "ARIMA", "NaiveMovingAverage", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_FT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "svr", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "CWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.2309,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.23) and high seasonality variance (0.62), with early horizon specialists (ONLY_DWT_svr, DWT_catboost) differing from late horizon models (FT_svr, ONLY_FT_catboost). The trend champion ONLY_FT_catboost captures downward trends best, while DWT_svr leads in seasonal correlation. Given the high disagreement and variance across models, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors without over-weighting any single approach."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage issues - baselines never leak, weighted/selection use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. n_windows=3 is low but current candidates are already in top ranking with score_margin_top2=0.007 between top2. Universe leaderboards show exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim0.6 has better RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE but current set already includes strong weighted candidate. No redundancy (no ≥4 same-type). All candidates valid from universe.candidate_names.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.23) and high seasonality variance (0.62), with early horizon specialists (ONLY_DWT_svr, DWT_catboost) differing from late horizon models (FT_svr, ONLY_FT_catboost). The trend champion ONLY_FT_catboost captures downward trends best, while DWT_svr leads in seasonal correlation. Given the high disagreement and variance across models, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model behaviors without over-weighting any single approach.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=11 indicates strong per-horizon model diversity, favoring selection methods like best_per_horizon_by_validation and topk_mean_per_horizon_k3. relative_spread_mean=0.4285 (≥0.25 threshold) justifies dba_combination for handling high disagreement. n_windows=3 is very small, so shrinkage=0.35 on inverse_rmse_weights provides stability. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for phase-shift robustness. RMSE spread ratio=1.23 indicates high model dispersion, supporting rmse_focus preset.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage issues - baselines never leak, weighted/selection use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. n_windows=3 is low but current candidates are already in top ranking with score_margin_top2=0.007 between top2. Universe leaderboards show exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim0.6 has better RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE but current set already includes strong weighted candidate. No redundancy (no ≥4 same-type). All candidates valid from universe.candidate_names.
